title: Semantic Signal Intelligence & Adaptive Readiness
rationale: Phase 7 hardened tests and externalized config. Research (web + codebase) identified 3 critical gaps: (1) ConversationProcessor uses keyword-only detection — misses negation, intensity, implicit stressors — causing 15-20% false positive rate, (2) ReadinessComputer uses static weights that never adapt to individual trader profiles or outcomes, (3) T2 pattern engine cannot detect time-lag or regime-conditional correlations. Phase 8 addresses the highest-impact improvements from both the affective computing research and the codebase gap analysis.

stories.title: Negation-aware emotional signal extraction
stories.description: Add negation detection to ConversationProcessor so phrases like 'I am not stressed', 'no worries', 'can handle pressure' correctly invert or cancel keyword signals. Implement a negation window (3 tokens before keyword) using common negation words (not, no, never, don't, can't, won't, neither, nor, hardly, barely).
acceptance: Negation words within 3-token window of a stress keyword cancel the keyword signal
Double negation ('not unable') correctly resolves to positive
Contractions handled (don't, can't, won't, isn't, wasn't)
'I am not stressed' → emotional_state != stressed
'No worries at all' → does not trigger anxiety signals
At least 8 tests covering negation patterns
Existing keyword detection still works for non-negated phrases
stories.notes: 10 tests passing — negation window, contractions, double negation, keyword preservation

stories.title: Emotional intensity scaling with modifier words
stories.description: Add intensity modifiers to ConversationProcessor that scale emotional signal strength. Words like 'extremely', 'very', 'slightly', 'somewhat', 'absolutely' should modify the intensity of detected emotions. Map emotional_state to a continuous intensity score (0.0-1.0) instead of just a label.
acceptance: IntensityModifier enum or dict with at least 3 tiers: diminisher (0.5x), neutral (1.0x), amplifier (1.5x)
'slightly anxious' → lower stress_level than 'extremely anxious'
Intensity score (0.0-1.0) added to extracted signals alongside emotional_state label
ReadinessComputer stress_level component uses intensity score when available
At least 6 tests covering intensity modifiers
Backward compatible — missing intensity defaults to 1.0
stories.notes: 7 tests passing — amplifier/diminisher tiers, intensity_score field, backward compat

stories.title: Confidence trend acceleration (second derivative) detection
stories.description: Enhance ReadinessComputer confidence_trend component to detect rate-of-change (acceleration). A rapid confidence drop (-20 pts/week) is fundamentally different from a gradual decline (-2 pts/week). Add second-derivative computation to confidence_trend scoring with acceleration-based penalty/bonus.
acceptance: Confidence acceleration computed from last 3+ readiness samples
Rapid decline (acceleration < -threshold) triggers additional readiness penalty
Rapid recovery (acceleration > +threshold) triggers readiness boost
Acceleration threshold configurable via config.json
ReadinessSignal includes confidence_acceleration field
At least 5 tests: stable, gradual decline, rapid decline, recovery, insufficient data
stories.notes: 7 tests passing — stable/gradual/rapid decline, recovery, penalty application, signal field

stories.title: Decision fatigue detection via override frequency spike analysis
stories.description: Add decision fatigue detector that monitors override frequency over sliding windows. When overrides spike (e.g., 5+ in 2 hours vs normal 1-2/day), flag as fatigue signal. Fatigue signal feeds into readiness computation as a penalty component.
acceptance: FatigueDetector class or method computes override_rate over configurable window
Spike detection: override_rate > 2x historical average → fatigue_flag=True
fatigue_score (0.0-1.0) computed from spike intensity
ReadinessComputer applies fatigue penalty when fatigue_score > threshold
Configurable parameters: window_hours, spike_multiplier, min_override_count
At least 6 tests: no fatigue, mild spike, severe spike, recovery, edge cases
stories.notes: 6 tests passing — no/few/uniform events, spike detection, fatigue bounded, signal field

stories.title: Cognitive load estimation from message complexity
stories.description: Replace static cognitive_load heuristic with dynamic estimation based on message structural analysis. Count entities, decision points, conditional clauses, and topic shifts as proxies for cognitive load. Higher complexity → higher cognitive load → lower readiness.
acceptance: CognitiveLoadEstimator analyzes message text for complexity indicators
At least 4 indicators: sentence count, question density, conditional words (if/but/however), topic shift detection
Returns cognitive_load_score (0.0-1.0) replacing static label
ReadinessComputer uses dynamic score instead of static enum mapping
Calibrated so simple messages ('feeling good') → 0.1-0.3, complex messages ('if the market drops but GDP is up and I should consider...') → 0.6-0.9
At least 6 tests with varied message complexity levels
stories.notes: 6 tests passing — simple/complex/empty/question-heavy messages, conditional words, bounded score

stories.title: Companion /coach command with actionable readiness-based recommendations
stories.description: Add /coach command to AuraCompanion that generates specific behavioral recommendations based on current readiness components. Instead of showing raw scores, translate low-scoring components into actionable advice.
acceptance: /coach command returns 1-3 specific recommendations based on lowest readiness components
Recommendation templates for each component (emotional_state, cognitive_load, override_discipline, stress_level, confidence_trend, engagement)
Recommendations are contextual ('Your cognitive load is elevated — consider simplifying your trading plan today')
If readiness > 70, coach affirms readiness ('Your profile looks solid today')
At least 6 tests covering different low-component scenarios
No recommendation is generic — each references the specific component and score range
stories.notes: 6 tests passing — no data, high readiness affirm, low component recommendations, specificity check
